content:
We place all circles above the horizontal line A (which we take to be y = 0) and to the right of P = (0,0).  Let the big circle B have centre (0,R) with R=2008, and for n≥1 let Cₙ have radius rₙ, centre (xₙ,rₙ), and satisfy

(1) tangency to the line y=0 ⇒ its centre has y-coordinate rₙ,
(2) tangency to B (externally) ⇒ dist((0,R),(xₙ,rₙ))=R+rₙ,
(3) tangency to the previous Cₙ₋₁ (externally) ⇒ dist((xₙ₋₁,rₙ₋₁),(xₙ,rₙ))=rₙ₋₁+rₙ.

From (2) one finds 
    xₙ²+(rₙ–R)²=(R+rₙ)²
⇒ xₙ=2√(R rₙ).
From (3) one finds, after some algebra,
    √rₙ – √rₙ₋₁ = √(rₙ rₙ₋₁⁄R).
Set aₙ=√rₙ and s=√R.  Then
    aₙ–aₙ₋₁ = aₙ aₙ₋₁/s,
so
    aₙ = s·aₙ₋₁/(s–aₙ₋₁).
If we normalize by bₙ = aₙ/s, this becomes
    bₙ = bₙ₋₁/(1–bₙ₋₁),
with b₁=1/s.  One checks by induction that
    bₙ = 1/(s–(n–1)),
so
    aₙ = s/(s–(n–1)),    rₙ = aₙ² = R/(s–(n–1))².

We need rₙ>0, i.e. s–(n–1)>0 ⇒ n–1<√R ⇒ n<√R+1.  Here √R=√2008≈44.82, so
    n < 45.82  ⇒  n ≤ 45.
Hence the largest n for which the construction still makes sense is

\boxed{45}.
